questions.question: Does having a 4K camera sell more software?

options.text: No, but poor audio/video creates 'Subconscious Friction' that lowers trust

questions.explanation: You don't need cinema quality, but if you look dark, grainy, and sound tinny, the prospect subconsciously assumes your product is also low-quality.

questions.question: What is the 'Eye Level' rule?

options.text: Your camera lens should be at hairline level, so you aren't looking down on the prospect

questions.explanation: If your laptop is on a low desk, you are looking down at the camera (the 'Nostril Shot'). This psychologically signals dominance or carelessness. Raise the laptop.

questions.question: What is the best way to handle 'Browser Clutter'?

options.text: Close all tabs except the specific ones needed for the demo, and use a dedicated 'Clean' browser profile
Keep Slack open to show you are busy

questions.explanation: A messy browser bar with 50 tabs signals chaos. Notifications popping up signal unprofessionalism. Create a specialized 'Demo Profile' that is pristine.

questions.question: How should you start the Zoom call?

options.text: Camera ON, smiling, ready 2 minutes early

questions.explanation: The first 3 seconds set the tone. If they log in and see a black screen or you fumbling with headphones, you start with a trust deficit.

questions.question: What is the 'Hardwired Rule'?

options.text: Always use an Ethernet cable if possible, because WiFi lag kills momentum

questions.explanation: A lagging screen share is the death of a demo. WiFi is unpredictable. For critical sales calls, being plugged in is an insurance policy.
